title: practical english usage - Part 9
content:
do, I telephoned Robin. See also co-ordinate clause, main clause, subordinate structures containing participles or infinitives. Example: Not knowing what to glad when Harry gets back. The  word clause is also sometimes used for some consist of one or more clauses. Examples: Alex couldn't come today. I'll be clause  a stretch of language which  contains a subject and a finite verb. Sentences singular). Examples: I'd like to phone; Pass the salt.

example in infinitives, imperatives and present tenses (except third person base form  the form of a verb that has no endings or other changes, used for See also modal auxiliary verb.

tenses, passive forms, etc. Examples: She was writing; Where have you put it?

auxiliary verb  a verb like be, have, do which  is used with another verb to make Examples: a green shirt; my noisy son. See also predicative.

attributive  Adjectives placed before nouns are in 'attributive position!

is often used to include aspect, for the sake of simplicity.

besides time (e.g. continuity, completion). However, in this book the term tense rather than progressive and perfect tense, since these forms express other ideas aspect  Many grammarians prefer to talk about progressive and perfective aspect, the is called the 'definite article'.

article  A, an and the are called 'articles' A/an is called the 'indefinite article’ ; an action is done by. Example: This picture was probably painted by a child.

agent  In a passive sentence, the agent is the expression that says who  or what agree (negative).

not a negative sentence or a question. Compare I agree (affirmative); I don't affirmative  an affirmative sentence is one that makes a positive statement - verb (e.g. clean up, look out, tell off).

adverb particle  a short adverb like up, out, off, often used as part of a phrasal I usually get up when I wake up; I'll phone you if I have time.

adverbial clause  a clause which  functions as an adverbial. Examples: On  Sundays adverb in a clause. Examples: I usually get up at seven o'clock on weekdays.

adverbial  an adverb, or a longer expression which  has a similar function to an for example, when, where or how  something happens.

adverb  a word like tomorrow, once, badly, there, also, which  is used to say, adjective clause  another name for relative clause nouns and pronouns. Examples: a green apple; She's hungry.

describe people, things, events, etc. Adjectives are used in connection with adjective  a word like green, hungry, impossible, which  is used when we responsible for what happens.

active verb is usually the person or thing that does the action, or that is was told, will be helped, which  are passive verb forms). The  subject of an active  An active verb form is one like breaks, told, will help (not like is broken, height; geography.

which  we  experience as an idea, not by seeing, touching, etc. Examples: doubt; abstract noun (the opposite of a concrete noun)  the name of something and other aspects of language.

The  following words and expressions are used in this book to talk about grammar Language       terminology Language terminology •  xxi direct object  see object.

include a/an, the, my, this, each, either, several, more, both, all.

determiner  one of a group of words that begin noun phrases. Determiners demonstrative  this, these, that, those.

defining relative  see identifying relative.

definite article  the.

statement. Example: That's your girlfriend?

declarative question  a question which  has the same grammatical form as a can be used with the indefinite article a/an. See also uncountable noun.

countable noun  a noun like car, dog, idea, which  can have a plural form, and co-ordinate structures. Examples: and, but, or.

co-ordinating conjunction  a conjunction that joins co-ordinate clauses or other subordinate clause.

It's cooler today and there's a bit of a wind. See also clause, main clause, Examples: Shall I come to your place or would you like to come to mine?; co-ordinate clause  one of two or more clauses of equal 'value' that are connected.
